chunks.page_summary: This page provides installation instructions for the TDM Add-in for Excel, which enables users to read TDMS files generated by Rheonics Inksight and SlurryTrack products.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

TDM Add-in for Excel: Installation Instructions

Modified on: Thu, 2 Jan, 2025 at 8:06 PM

What products are involved?
TDMS files generated by Rheonics Inksight and SlurryTrack.

What is the purpose of this article?
This article describes the procedure for installing an Excel TDM Add-In for to read TDMS files.

1. Overview
TDMS files are a binary file format developed by National Instruments (NI) for storing and managing large volumes of measurement data. Rheonics Inksight and SlurryTrack log job data in this format. One way to access this data is by using the TDM Add-in for Excel. This article provides a step-by-step guide on how to download, install, and use the TDM Add-in for Excel from NI.

2. TDM Add-In Download
The TDM Add-In for Excel can be downloaded from National Instruments website (https://www.ni.com/en/support/downloads/tools-network/download.tdm-excel-add-in-for-microsoft-excel.html#378046), however an account is needed.
key_facts: The document provides installation instructions for the TDM Add-in for Excel.
The TDM Add-in for Excel allows users to read TDMS files.
TDMS files are generated by Rheonics Inksight and Rheonics SlurryTrack products.
TDMS is a binary file format developed by National Instruments (NI) for storing and managing large volumes of measurement data.
Rheonics Inksight and SlurryTrack log job data in the TDMS format.
The TDM Add-in for Excel can be downloaded from the National Instruments website.
warnings_notes: An account is needed to download the TDM Add-In for Excel from the National Instruments website.

Figure 1. National Instruments TDM Excel Add-In Download: Account Needed

After creating the account, the download option becomes available. Since the NI Package Manager is necessary to install the Add-In, it is recommended to select the “Install Offline” option (see Figure 2). This option will install both the Package Manager and the Add-In for Excel one after the other.
key_facts: The TDM Excel Add-In from National Instruments allows users to load and work with TDM and TDMS files directly within Microsoft Excel.
The latest version of the Add-In was released on November 24, 2021.
The software supports Windows operating systems and is compatible with both 32-bit and 64-bit applications.
A National Instruments account is required, and users must log in to download the Add-In.
The NI Package Manager (NIPM) must be installed prior to the TDM Excel Add-In.
It is recommended to use the "Install Offline" option, which will install both the NI Package Manager and the Add-In sequentially.

warnings_notes: Note: Install NI Package Manager (NIPM) before installing this product. NIPM helps you install, upgrade, and manage NI software.
After creating the account, the download option becomes available.
It is recommended to select the "Install Offline" option (see Figure 2) for installation, which installs both the Package Manager and the Add-In for Excel one after the other.

3. TDM Add-In Installation

The downloaded ISO file needs to be "mounted" first. To do this, right-click the ISO file and select "Mount."
key_facts: The TDM Excel Add-In enables users to load and work with TDM and TDMS files in Microsoft Excel.
The installation file for the TDM Excel Add-In is provided as an ISO file.
To install the add-in, the downloaded ISO file needs to be mounted by right-clicking it and selecting 'Mount'.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions on how to use an Excel Add-In to open TDM and TDMS files and set it as the default application.
chunks.extracted_text:
4. Open TDM Files
With the Add-In installed, the Add-Ins tab should now be visible in the Excel Ribbon, as shown in Figure 11.
Figure 11. Excel Add-Ins tab
To open a TDMS file, select the TDM Importer option inside that Add-ins tab. From there, you can browse and open a TDMS file
Figure 12. TDM Import option in Excel
The Add-In can be set as the default application to open TDMS files. To do this, right-click on a TDMS file, select Properties, and click Change under the Opens with section. Then, select “niskyline_exceladdinimporter” as the default application, as shown in the image.
key_facts: The Add-In, once installed, makes the 'Add-Ins' tab visible in the Excel Ribbon.
To open a TDMS file, users must select the 'TDM Importer' option from the 'Add-Ins' tab.
The Add-In can be configured as the default application for opening TDMS files by selecting 'niskyline_exceladdinimporter' via file properties.
diagram_labels: Figure 12. TDM Import option in Excel

key_facts: Rheonics TDMS files contain specific application-logged data, including measurements, alarms, events, and diagnostic information.
The 'niskyline_exceladdinimporter' is suggested as a default application for opening .tdms files.

The TDMS file structure includes channels for various parameters such as Absolute Time, Relative Timestamp, Temperature, Density, Viscosity, Frequency, Compensated Temperature, Setpoint, and various control states.
Measurements in the TDMS file are recorded with units such as seconds (s), degrees Celsius (°C), kilograms per cubic meter (kg/m3), millipascal-seconds (mPa.s), and Hertz (Hz).
The software used to log data in the TDMS file is 'Rheonics InkSight'.
diagram_labels: How do you want to open .tdms files from now on?
Look for an app in the Microsoft Store
